dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6239 IH: Made in America Integrity Act of 2025
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Van Duyne introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Small Business

form.official-title: To amend the Small Business Act to require penalties for small business concerns falsely claiming goods or services are Made in America, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Made in America Integrity Act of 2025.

section.header: Penalties for small business concerns falsely claiming goods or services are Made in America
section.text: Section 16 of the Small Business Act (15 U.S.C. 645) is amended by adding at the end the following new subsection:

subsection.header: False claims of Made in America
subsection.text: In addition to any other applicable penalties, a small business concern that is awarded a Federal contract in part or in whole because the concern falsely claims the goods or services that are the subject of the contract are made or produced in the United States shall be subject to the penalties and remedies described in subsection (d)(2), except that subparagraph (D) of such subsection shall be applied to such concern by substituting 5 years for 3 years.
